sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Insider Sell: van der Velden Jan Sells 23,000 Shares of Darling Ingredients Inc (DAR)
feed.summary: van der Velden Jan, Executive Vice President of International Rendering and Specialties at Darling Ingredients Inc (DAR), sold 23,000 shares of the company on February 18, 2026. This transaction leaves him with 71,678 shares. Over the past year, there have been no insider buys and seven insider sells at Darling Ingredients Inc. The article also notes that Darling Ingredients Inc stock, trading at $51.23, is significantly overvalued with a price-to-GF-Value ratio of 1.32.

feed.title: News Corp (NASDAQ: NWSA) updates progress on US$1B share repurchase
feed.summary: News Corp (NASDAQ: NWSA) has provided an update on its US$1 billion share repurchase program for Class A and Class B common stock. The company has actively bought back shares, totaling over 1.5 million Class A shares and approximately 719,000 Class B shares as of February 19, 2026, for a combined value of over $58 million. The repurchases are made through Goldman Sachs & Co. LLC, with prices ranging from $22.20 to $31.40 per share, demonstrating ongoing capital deployment to enhance shareholder value.

feed.title: Build-to-suit industrial property near O'Hare sells to Realty Income
feed.summary: A 67,000-square-foot build-to-suit industrial property called Cubes at ORD, developed by CRG near O'Hare, has been sold to Realty Income Corp. The property includes a tenant lease extending through 2030. This sale highlights continued activity in the Chicago commercial real estate market, particularly for industrial assets.

feed.title: Eli Lilly (LLY) Expands Pipeline with Clazakizumab Agreement, Reuters Reports
feed.summary: Eli Lilly (LLY) has expanded its pipeline by securing development and commercialization rights for clazakizumab, an anti-interleukin-6 monoclonal antibody, from Australia’s CSL. This agreement includes an upfront payment of $100 million to CSL and potential additional milestone payments and royalties. Separately, Eli Lilly has built up $1.5 billion in pre-launch inventory for its experimental oral weight-loss drug, orforglipron, ahead of a possible FDA decision in April.

feed.title: (VZLA) Trading Performance and Risk Management (VZLA:CA)
feed.summary: This article provides trading performance and risk management insights for Vizsla Silver Corp (VZLA:CA), including short-term and long-term ratings. It suggests a long-term buy strategy near 3.48 with a stop loss at 3.46, and highlights that AI-generated signals and real-time triggers are available. The analysis also lists numerous recent articles offering various trading advice and reports for VZLA:CA.

feed.title: AMETEK Inc.: Why This ‘Boring’ Stock Just Got Seriously Interesting
feed.summary: AMETEK Inc. is presented as a "stealth winner" in the industrial-tech sector, quietly achieving record earnings and beating Wall Street expectations by focusing on high-margin technology for essential industries. The article highlights AMETEK's consistent revenue growth, expanding margins, and strategic acquisitions in niche high-tech businesses, positioning it as a stable, long-term growth play for investors seeking diversification beyond speculative hype. While not a "to the moon" stock, its critical role in various industrial applications and US policy tailwinds make it an interesting opportunity for serious investors.

feed.title: Wells Fargo Raises Outlook on Cardinal Health (CAH), Cites Broad Business Strength
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has raised its price target for Cardinal Health (CAH) to $256 from $237, reiterating an Overweight rating following strong Q2 performance and increased earnings estimates. The firm highlighted broad business strength, particularly in Pharmaceutical and Specialty segments, with momentum expected to continue. Cardinal Health also raised its fiscal 2026 profit outlook, driven by strong demand for specialty medicines, though GLP-1 drugs are not yet expected to significantly impact near-term profits.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Upgrades Merck (MRK) to Buy, Sees "Clear Path" Beyond Keytruda Patent Cliff
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has upgraded Merck (MRK) to Buy from Hold, with analyst James Shin increasing the price target to $150 from $115. Shin believes the market is undervaluing Merck due to concerns about Keytruda's patent expiration, but sees a "clear path" for the company beyond this cliff and identifies visible growth drivers outside of Keytruda. This upgrade comes despite Merck's earlier 2026 forecast that fell below Wall Street expectations due to the loss of exclusivity for Januvia and other older medicines.

feed.title: News | Saks Global and its biggest landlord, Simon Property, clash over two stores
feed.summary: Saks Global, currently in Chapter 11 bankruptcy, has resolved most disputes over unpaid rent with landlords and secured $1.75 billion in financing. However, it continues to clash with its largest landlord, Simon Property Group, over the latter's attempt to terminate two store leases to relet them at higher rates. This dispute highlights the complex relationship between the two companies, who are also partners in a joint venture.

feed.title: 3 Under-the-Radar AI Infrastructure Stocks Powering the Next Buildout
feed.summary: The article highlights three under-the-radar AI infrastructure stocks—Prologis (PLD), Gladstone Land (LAND), and Black Hills Corporation (BKH)—as crucial players in the ongoing AI boom. These companies provide essential resources like land, power, and water, which are vital for the expansion of data centers, distinguishing them from the high-profile "Mag 7" tech stocks. The author suggests these "picks and shovels" companies offer a more stable investment approach to the AI economy.

feed.title: Intel's next chapter in Oregon: What it means for jobs, investment and the state economy
feed.summary: Intel, Oregon's largest private employer, faces scrutiny after deep layoffs despite its historical role in the state's Silicon Forest. The company's director of U.S. government and community affairs, Courtney Martin, discusses the company's future outlook, federal investment, and Nvidia's $5 billion stake, emphasizing Intel's commitment to innovation, talent retention through partnerships with local universities, and its continued importance to Oregon's economy amidst global competition.

feed.title: McDonald's (MCD) Upgraded to Buy by Argus on Strong Competitive Positioning
feed.summary: Argus analyst John Staszak upgraded McDonald's (MCD) to Buy from Hold with a $380 price target, citing its strong competitive position through value menus, digital investments, and new product launches. This upgrade follows a positive response from Wall Street analysts to the company's Q4 2025 earnings report, with some maintaining a bullish outlook for the stock. JPMorgan and Deutsche Bank also hold positive ratings, emphasizing the success of McDonald's value strategy and its efforts to recapture market attention.

feed.title: Evercore ISI Raises Costco (COST) Price Target, Citi Also Lifts Valuation After Q2 Beat
feed.summary: Evercore ISI and Citi analysts have both raised their price targets for Costco (COST) following the company's strong fiscal Q2 performance. Costco reported a modest beat in same-store sales and earnings, with notable growth in e-commerce and a significant increase in membership fee income and total paid members. The article also touches upon Costco's business model and briefly mentions other investment opportunities in AI stocks.

feed.title: Is Archrock (AROC) Outperforming Other Oils-Energy Stocks This Year?
feed.summary: Archrock Inc. (AROC) is showing strong performance in the Oils-Energy sector this year, with a 26.3% gain compared to the sector's average of 20.1%. The company holds a Zacks Rank of #1 (Strong Buy) due to improving analyst sentiment. While outperforming its sector, AROC is slightly underperforming its specific industry, Oil and Gas - Field Services, which has grown by 33.6%.

feed.title: Insider Selling: US Foods (NYSE:USFD) Insider Sells $6,344,935.24 in Stock
feed.summary: US Foods (NYSE:USFD) director James David Works Jr. sold 65,737 shares of the company's stock for over $6.3 million, reducing his stake by 42.71%. This sale follows the company's recent earnings report, which met EPS expectations but slightly missed revenue estimates. Analysts currently rate US Foods as a "Moderate Buy" with an average target price of $107.33.

feed.title: Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) Insider Christopher Scott Edmonds Sells 11,303 Shares
feed.summary: Intercontinental Exchange (NYSE:ICE) insider Christopher Scott Edmonds sold 11,303 shares of the company's stock for approximately $1.74 million, reducing his position by 44.21%. This sale was disclosed in an SEC filing and leaves him with 14,266 shares. The company recently reported strong quarterly results, beating EPS and revenue expectations, and holds a consensus "Buy" rating from analysts with an average price target of $195.40.

feed.title: Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX) SVP Sells $196,227.14 in Stock
feed.summary: Patrick Plewman, an SVP at Quest Diagnostics (NYSE:DGX), sold 958 shares of the company's stock for $196,227.14, reducing his stake by 5.7%. This transaction follows a previous sale of 5,662 shares for $1.16 million earlier in the month. The news comes after Quest Diagnostics reported stronger-than-expected quarterly earnings and revenue, approved a $1.0 billion share buyback, and increased its quarterly dividend.

feed.title: Deutsche Bank Upgrades Merck (MRK) to Buy, Sees “Clear Path” Beyond Keytruda Patent Cliff
feed.summary: Deutsche Bank has upgraded Merck (MRK) to Buy from Hold, raising its price target to $150 from $115, citing a "clear path" for the company beyond the upcoming patent expiration of its blockbuster drug, Keytruda. Analyst James Shin believes the market is undervaluing Merck, emphasizing that the company possesses other visible growth drivers and is poised for trough earnings. Despite a recent lower-than-expected 2026 forecast from Merck due to patent expirations for older drugs like Januvia, the analyst maintains a positive outlook.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Unum Group (NYSE:UNM) Director Sells 9,000 Shares of Stock
feed.summary: Unum Group (NYSE:UNM) Director Cynthia Egan recently sold 9,000 shares of the company's stock for $657,000, reducing her ownership by over 17%. This sale follows the company missing Q4 earnings estimates and issuing FY2026 guidance lower than analyst expectations, despite announcing a $1 billion share buyback and a quarterly dividend.

feed.title: When (VOXR) Moves Investors should Listen (VOXR:CA)
feed.summary: This article provides trading analysis and signals for Vox Royalty Corp. (VOXR:CA). It suggests a long-term buy near 4.40 with a stop loss at 4.38 and indicates strong ratings across near, mid, and long terms. The article also lists various past reports related to VOXR, highlighting continuous coverage and strategic insights for investors.

feed.title: Lowe's (LOW) Workforce Reduction Comes as Bernstein Maintains Bullish Rating
feed.summary: Lowe's Companies, Inc. announced plans to cut approximately 600 corporate and support positions, less than 1% of its workforce, to focus more on store operations and customer interaction. Concurrently, Bernstein raised its price target for Lowe's to $313 from $284 while reiterating an Outperform rating, despite anticipating modest Q4 earnings due to winter storms. The company stated it would provide financial support and career assistance to affected employees.

feed.title: Q&A & Video: Leigh Tiffin Explains Decision to Join NIRVC
feed.summary: Leigh Tiffin, former president of Tiffin Motorhomes, discusses his decision to join National Indoor RV Centers (NIRVC) as its new president. He explains that the move was driven by a "phenomenal personal opportunity," including NIRVC's ambitious goal of an initial public offering (IPO). Tiffin emphasizes that his departure from Tiffin Motorhomes was not due to negative reasons, but rather a pursuit of a unique career path.

feed.title: Viavi launches ePRTC360+ clock alternative to Cesium-accuracy holdover clock
feed.summary: Viavi Solutions Inc. has introduced its patent-pending Cesium-less ePRTC360+ holdover solution, offering an alternative to Cesium atomic clocks for maintaining precise timing. This new technology meets the ITU-T G.8272.1 standard for Enhanced Primary Reference Time Clocks (ePRTC) and provides resilient timing against GNSS disruptions without the drawbacks of Cesium clocks. The ePRTC360+ will be demonstrated at Mobile World Congress (MWC) Barcelona 2026.

feed.title: PepsiCo Sued Over Attempt to Bury PETA Shareholder Resolution Over Abuse of Bulls
feed.summary: PETA Foundation has filed a lawsuit against PepsiCo for trying to block a shareholder resolution concerning animal treatment in its supply chains, specifically regarding the abuse of bulls in India. The resolution seeks a report on the treatment of these animals, which are forced to haul overloaded carts, often suffering severe injuries and handler abuse. PETA highlights that mechanized solutions like eco-tractors exist and improve efficiency, but PepsiCo has failed to address the documented cruelty despite being urged by over 50,000 supporters.

feed.title: World Kinect Financial Results: Q4 and Annual Loss Reported for Fiscal Year - News and Statistics
feed.summary: World Kinect Corporation reported a significant fourth-quarter loss of $279.7 million, or $5.11 per share, falling short of analyst expectations. For the full fiscal year, the company posted an annual loss of $614.4 million on revenues of $36.92 billion. Despite the losses, World Kinect generated $9.03 billion in quarterly revenue and provided a forward-looking earnings per share guidance of $2.20 to $2.40 for the coming year.

feed.title: Caterpillar (CAT) Price Target Raised by BofA as Turbine Demand Broadens
feed.summary: BofA recently raised its price target for Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) to $825 from $735, reiterating a Buy rating. The increase is driven by broadening turbine demand beyond data centers and "eyepopping growth" in Caterpillar's power generation unit, including a 24% year-over-year rise in oil and gas demand. Caterpillar is benefiting from AI growth through its construction equipment for data centers and its Power & Energy segment providing stable and backup power solutions.

feed.title: (URC) Comprehensive Trading Strategy Report (URC:CA)
feed.summary: This report provides a comprehensive trading strategy for Uranium Royalty Corp. (URC:CA), recommending a long position near 3.58 with a stop loss at 3.56. It includes AI-generated ratings for February 20, indicating strong near-term performance but weak mid and long-term outlooks, and suggests checking for real-time triggers. The article also lists recent related analyses for URC:CA.

feed.title: Caterpillar (CAT) Price Target Raised by BofA as Turbine Demand Broadens
feed.summary: BofA has raised its price target for Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) from $735 to $825, reiterating a Buy rating, due to broadening turbine demand beyond data centers. The firm noted "eyepopping growth" in Caterpillar's power generation unit and an increase in oil and gas demand. Caterpillar is benefiting from AI growth, with its equipment used in data center construction and its Power & Energy segment providing essential turbines and engines.

feed.title: Caterpillar (CAT) Price Target Raised by BofA as Turbine Demand Broadens
feed.summary: BofA has raised its price target for Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) to $825 from $735, reiterating a Buy rating. The increase is driven by expanding turbine demand beyond data centers, significant growth in Caterpillar's power generation unit, and rising oil and gas demand. The company also reported record revenue of $67.6 billion, with strong performance in its Power & Energy segment, and a record backlog of $51.2 billion, despite a decline in full-year EPS due to higher incremental costs.

feed.title: VSee Health Invests in GoMyRx Digital Prescription Platform
feed.summary: VSee Health has entered into a managed services agreement with GoMyRx, a digital prescription fulfillment platform, to provide administrative and support services. The agreement, effective December 26, 2025, extends for two years. Additionally, VSee Health deepened its partnership by purchasing a 10% equity stake in GoMyRx for $2 million in January 2026.

feed.title: Mizuho Raises Sherwin-Williams (SHW) Price Target to $410 after Earnings Review
feed.summary: Mizuho has increased its price target for Sherwin-Williams (SHW) to $410 from $400, maintaining an Outperform rating, following a review of the company's latest earnings. Sherwin-Williams reported record consolidated sales and adjusted diluted EPS for the full year 2025, alongside its 47th consecutive year of dividend increases. The company also highlighted strategic investments, the acquisition of Suvinil, and a commitment to long-term growth.

feed.title: SmartStop Self Storage REIT Announces Appointment of New Management Board Member
feed.summary: SmartStop Self Storage REIT, Inc. has announced the appointment of Wayne Johnson, their Chief Investment Officer, to its Board of Directors. This move recognizes Johnson's extensive experience and leadership in the self-storage and commercial real estate sectors, where he has been instrumental in SmartStop's growth since 2013. His expertise is expected to further advance the company's long-term strategy and explore new opportunities.

feed.title: Don’t overreact: Tariffs overturned, but fundamentals still rule
feed.summary: The article discusses the Supreme Court's decision to strike down the president's use of IEEPA to implement tariffs, arguing that this will likely have limited impact on long-term intrinsic valuations, similar to the initial tariff announcements. It highlights that market fundamentals and company-specific issues, rather than tariffs, are the primary drivers of stock performance, citing examples like Nike, Walmart, and Apple. The author also notes the surprising strength of the US economy in 2025 despite the tariffs, attributing it to other factors such as the AI boom and consumer spending.

feed.title: TD Cowen Flags Margin and Comparable Sales Pressure at Tractor Supply (TSCO)
feed.summary: TD Cowen has lowered its price recommendation for Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) to $53 from $55, reiterating a Hold rating due to a difficult operating environment squeezing comparable sales and margins. The company missed Wall Street expectations for fourth-quarter net sales and issued a weaker-than-expected outlook for annual sales and profit, citing a shift in consumer behavior towards essential products over discretionary items. Higher import costs (tariffs) also impacted gross margins, leading the company to adjust pricing strategies.

feed.title: AM Best Upgrades Credit Ratings for RLI Corp. and Its Subsidiaries
feed.summary: AM Best has upgraded the Financial Strength Rating (FSR) to A++ (Superior) from A+ (Superior) and the Long-Term Issuer Credit Ratings (Long-Term ICR) to “aa+” (Superior) from “aa” (Superior) for the members of RLI Group. The publicly traded parent company, RLI Corp. [NYSE: RLI], also received an upgrade to its Long-Term ICR to “a+” (Excellent) from “a” (Excellent). These upgrades reflect RLI's exceptional balance sheet strength, strong operating performance, and effective capital management strategies, with a stable outlook reflecting expectations for continued strong profitability.

feed.title: Insider Selling: Steven Mizell Sells 1,400 Shares of Allegion PL
feed.summary: Allegion PLC Director Steven Mizell sold 1,400 shares of the company on February 19, 2026, and now owns 4,915 shares. The stock is currently trading at $162.16, making it modestly overvalued according to its GF Value of $146.85, and its price-earnings ratio of 21.94 is higher than the industry median. Over the past year, Allegion PLC has seen 1 insider buy and 7 insider sells.

feed.title: Summit Therapeutics Set to Announce Q4 Earnings on February 23
feed.summary: Summit Therapeutics (SMMT) is scheduled to release its Q4 earnings on February 23, with an expected EPS of -$0.02, signaling a significant year-over-year improvement. Despite historical challenges in meeting revenue estimates, the stock has seen a spike and rating upgrades due to its lead asset undergoing FDA review, suggesting cautious market optimism. Analysts have mixed views, with some revising EPS estimates upward while revenue estimates have faced a downward revision.

feed.title: Western Copper and Gold Files Final Prospectus for C$80 Million Bought Deal Offering
feed.summary: Western Copper and Gold has filed a final prospectus for a public offering of common shares valued at C$80 million, with a potential increase to C$92 million. This financing aims to support the advancement of its flagship Casino copper-gold project in Yukon. The offering is expected to close around February 26, 2026, pending regulatory approvals.

feed.title: United Rentals Stock: Quiet Boom Play Every Young Investor Is Missing
feed.summary: The article highlights United Rentals Inc. (URI) as an overlooked investment opportunity, contrasting it with popular AI and meme stocks. It emphasizes URI's role in supplying rental equipment for America's construction and infrastructure boom, driven by government spending and reshoring. The company is characterized by strong revenue growth, high margins, and shareholder-friendly actions like buybacks, though it acknowledges the cyclical risks associated with the construction industry.

feed.title: Hormel announces definitive agreement to sell its whole-bird turkey business
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has entered into a definitive agreement to sell its whole-bird turkey business to Life-Science Innovations (LSI). The sale, expected to close by the end of Q2 2026, includes the Melrose production facility, Swanville feed mill, and associated transport assets, but excludes JENNIE-O branded products. Hormel aims to focus on value-added protein while LSI will take over supply contracts and provide co-manufacturing services during the transition.

feed.title: ORR ETF Blows Past Market With Strong 2026 Returns
feed.summary: The Militia Long/Short Equity ETF (ORR), managed by David Orr, has shown strong performance, gaining 13.4% in 2026 after a 32.2% increase in 2025. The ETF has attracted significant investor capital, reaching $443 million in assets, by leveraging a long/short strategy that seeks to exploit market anomalies in less volatile and small-cap stocks. Despite a high headline expense ratio, most of it reflects operational mechanics rather than management fees, and ORR provides a publicly accessible version of Orr's successful hedge fund strategy, offering lower correlation and beta to the market.

feed.title: Barclays Raises Illinois Tool Works (ITW) Price Target, Sees “Some Encouragement” in Outlook
feed.summary: Barclays analyst Julian Mitchell increased his price target for Illinois Tool Works (ITW) to $275 from $244, maintaining an Underweight rating despite seeing "some encouragement" in the company's outlook. This follows Illinois Tool Works' strong fourth-quarter results, which exceeded Wall Street expectations with earnings of $2.72 per share and $4.09 billion in revenue, driven by steady automotive demand and tariff mitigation efforts. However, the company's 2026 EPS forecast midpoint of $11-$11.40 is slightly below current analyst estimates.

feed.title: RBC Capital Maintains Sector Perform on Obsidian Energy Ltd (OBE) Feb 2026
feed.summary: RBC Capital has maintained its "Sector Perform" rating on Obsidian Energy Ltd (OBE) and increased its price target to C$10 from C$9. This indicates a cautious but slightly more optimistic outlook based on RBC's view of near-term cash flow and commodity assumptions. Investors should interpret the "Sector Perform" rating as neutral, expecting OBE to perform in line with its peers, while the price target increase suggests marginally stronger fundamentals without a full upgrade.

feed.title: TD Cowen Flags Margin and Comparable Sales Pressure at Tractor Supply (TSCO)
feed.summary: TD Cowen has lowered its price recommendation for Tractor Supply Company (TSCO) to $53 from $55, maintaining a Hold rating due to margin and comparable sales pressure. The company faces a challenging operating environment with softer demand for discretionary items, leading to missed Q4 sales and profit expectations and a conservative fiscal 2026 outlook. Tariffs also impacted gross margins, despite price adjustments and promotions, as consumer behavior shifts towards essential spending.

feed.title: Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE:RCL) Director Sells $31,802,595.18 in Stock
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean Cruises (NYSE:RCL) Director Arne Alexander Wilhelmsen sold 101,902 shares of the company's stock on February 19th for over $31.8 million, following other sales earlier in the month totaling approximately $156.6 million. Despite these sales, Wilhelmsen retains a significant direct ownership of over 17.6 million shares valued at about $5.52 billion. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend and authorized a $2.0 billion share buyback program.

feed.title: Businesses brace for impact ahead of York Water rate increase
feed.summary: York businesses are preparing for a significant increase in water bills starting March 1 due to a rate hike by The York Water Company. This increase will raise average residential bills by $34 monthly, putting financial pressure on both local residents and businesses. The article highlights the impending impact of these higher costs on the community.

feed.title: York Water rate hike: How it impacts local businesses
feed.summary: The York Water Company's rate increase, effective March 1, is set to raise average residential bills by approximately $34 per month, significantly impacting local businesses as well. Establishments like Gift Horse Brewing Company and World A Cuts barbershop anticipate higher operating costs, with some considering increasing menu prices or facing doubled water bills due to increased usage and new programs. The company's CEO states the rate hike balances investment in their systems with customer impact, a move businesses hope will maintain infrastructure for future stability while acknowledging the current financial pressure.

feed.title: THC: Leerink Partners Raises Price Target for Tenet Healthcare
feed.summary: Leerink Partners has reaffirmed its "Outperform" rating for Tenet Healthcare (THC) and increased its price target from $255.00 to $264.00, indicating a positive outlook for the company's future growth. This is part of a trend of favorable analyst ratings and increased price targets from various firms such as Truist Securities, Guggenheim, Barclays, Keybanc, and UBS. While analysts project an average upside of 8.71% from the current price, GuruFocus estimates a potential downside of 37.11% based on its GF Value fair value assessment.

feed.title: Ryder System (NYSE:R) EVP Sells $597,460.56 in Stock
feed.summary: Rajeev Ravindran, EVP of Ryder System (NYSE:R), sold 2,696 shares of company stock for $597,460.56 on February 19th, further reducing his stake by 12.94% to 18,136 shares. This follows a previous sale of 14,367 shares on February 13th, bringing his total sales to approximately $3.64 million. The company recently missed quarterly earnings estimates, reporting $3.59 EPS against an expected $3.66 EPS, and announced a quarterly dividend of $0.91.

feed.title: PTC Therapeutics (NASDAQ:PTCT) CFO Pierre Gravier Sells 2,992 Shares
feed.summary: PTC Therapeutics' CFO, Pierre Gravier, sold 2,992 shares of the company's stock for approximately $207,525 on February 18th, reducing his stake by 3.31%. This insider sale follows a significant fourth-quarter earnings miss, where the company reported an EPS of ($1.67) against an estimated ($0.21) and revenue of $164.7 million versus a $281.5 million consensus, leading to lowered FY-2026 revenue guidance. Despite these financial setbacks and regulatory uncertainties, the company highlights strong early uptake of Sephience and a robust cash position of $1.95 billion to support future commercialization and R&D.
